phase-1.name: USB Composite PoC
phase-1.description: HID keyboard + CDC-ACM serial proof of concept

FR1.title: USB HID Boot Keyboard
FR1.description: Emulate a USB HID boot protocol keyboard recognized by BIOS and OS

FR2.title: USB CDC-ACM Serial
FR2.description: Expose a CDC-ACM serial port for bidirectional communication with host

FR3.title: ASCII String Injection
FR3.description: Type arbitrary ASCII strings via HID keyboard with correct US QWERTY keycodes

FR4.title: CDC Echo Demo
FR4.description: Echo received CDC data back to host as basic bidirectional verification

FR5.title: Visual Status LED
FR5.description: WS2812 RGB LED indicating device state (boot, USB ready, BIOS mode, error, etc.)

phase-2.name: WiFi + Command Execution
phase-2.description: Connect to hotspot and execute diagnostic commands

FR2.1.title: WiFi Hotspot Connection
FR2.1.description: Connect to pre-configured WiFi hotspot on device boot

FR2.2.title: CDC Serial Operator Input
FR2.2.description: Accept operator command input via CDC serial port

FR2.3.title: HID Command Injection
FR2.3.description: Inject commands received over CDC into target via HID keyboard

FR2.4.title: NVS State Machine
FR2.4.description: NVS-persisted state machine for multi-step workflows across power cycles

FR2.5.title: WiFi LED Feedback
FR2.5.description: Visual LED feedback during WiFi connection and command execution phases

phase-4.name: CDC-NCM USB Ethernet
phase-4.description: Replace CDC-ACM with CDC-NCM for USB Ethernet networking
